input.prompt: Your team is upgrading the codebase from .NET 9 to .NET 10. You have been asked to benchmark AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount to check how the upgrade will impact their performance. The source code is in the TextAnalyzer subdirectory (TextAnalyzer.cs and TextAnalyzer.csproj). We don't need anything too comprehensive, don't spend more than 10 minutes on this. Don't worry about cleaning up any files or projects you create along the way. Note: this machine may be under load from other processes. If the .NET 9 runtime is missing, install it using the official dotnet-install script from https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh (or dotnet-install.ps1 on Windows).

data.content: The benchmark only ran on .NET 10. Let me fix the project to properly multi-target with BenchmarkDotNet job attributes.
data.reasoningText:
It only ran for net10.0. The `--runtimes net9.0 net10.0` flag didn't seem to create separate jobs for both runtimes. Let me check if BenchmarkDotNet actually ran both. Looking at the output, it says "DefaultJob" with .NET 10.0.7 only. The `--runtimes` flag with BenchmarkDotNet uses MonoRuntime/CoreRuntime, and I think I need to specify it differently or use `[SimpleJob]` attributes.

Let me look at what happened. The `--runtimes` argument for BenchmarkDotNet CLI typically takes values like `net9.0`, `net10.0` - but this only works if the project actually targets those frameworks. 

I had `TargetFrameworks` set to both, but I ran with `-f net10.0`, which locked it to that framework. The correct runtime monikers for BenchmarkDotNet might be different—something like `net90` and `net100` instead. Let me try running without the framework filter and see if BenchmarkDotNet picks up both targets properly.

data.content: BDN 0.14.0 doesn't know about .NET 10 yet. I'll run separate benchmarks for each TFM and compare.
data.reasoningText:
BDN 0.14.0 doesn't have Net100 moniker yet since .NET 10 is relatively new. Let me check what the latest version of BenchmarkDotNet is, or try a different approach. I could use a nightly build, or I could just run the benchmarks manually without BDN's multi-targeting.

Alternative approach: Run the benchmark twice — once with `-f net9.0` and once with `-f net10.0` using only the default job, and compare results manually.

Let me simplify.

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-19fa3a3df7a947f8a6345a5bf901ba82/Benchmarks && rm -rf BenchmarkDotNet.Artifacts && dotnet run -c Release -f net9.0 --no-launch-profile -- --join --artifactsPath ./artifacts-net9 2>&1
cd /tmp/sv-19fa3a3df7a947f8a6345a5bf901ba82/Benchmarks && rm -rf BenchmarkDotNet.Artifacts && dotnet run -c Release -f net9.0 --no-launch-profile -- --join --artifactsPath ./artifacts-net9 2>&1
